title: Safety control module for a robot assembly and method of same
abstract: A robot assembly for safe operation in a manufacturing setting with humans including a sensor for detecting a human location and human movement is provided. A safety control module providing a boundary of a safety zone area that is associated with the human in a task oriented state that includes a largest possible area in which the human or an associated work object can extend when the human is standing in one location and performing the work task. The human movement and safety zone area location being used to develop a capture set area that includes at least one predictive future safety zone area location. Using the at least one predicted future safety zone area, establishing a travel path for moving the robot between locations without overlapping the capture set area.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: Claim 1. A method for operating a robot assembly to prevent collision with a human in a manufacturing setting comprising: providing a robot assembly with a safety control module; with a sensor determining a human location; determining with the safety control module at least one safety zone area to avoid corresponding to a work task assigned to the human which includes the area the human or an associated work object could extend when the human is standing in one location and performing the work task; and preventing the robot assembly from moving within the safety zone area. Claim 2. The method of Claim 1, including a step of determining a capture set area that includes changes in the safety zone area as a function of time. Claim 3. The method of Claim 2, wherein the step of determining the capture set area includes predicting at least one future location of the safety zone area based on patterned movement of the safety zone area as the human travels between locations. Claim 4. The method of Claim 3, wherein if a programed movement path of the robot assembly intersects the capture set determining an override path to avoid the capture set area to avoid the future location of the human. Claim 5. The method of Claim 4, wherein determining the override path includes determining the most direct route over a predetermined time interval for avoiding the capture set. Claim 6. The method of Claim 5, wherein the override path includes speed and direction of travel of the robot assembly. Claim 7. The method of Claim 4, wherein the override path information is used by a C.P.U. on the robot assembly as executable instructions to move the robot assembly along the override path until the human has moved away from a potentially overlapping position of the programed movement path. Claim 8. The method of Claim 7, including continuously developing the capture set area to change the override path back to the programed movement path if human movement does not follow predicted movement. Claim 9. The method of Claim 7, wherein assigned work task data corresponding to a specific human or a specific location is stored in a memory unit in communication with the C.P.U. to help develop the capture set area. Claim 10. The method of Claim 1, wherein in the event the sensor cannot determine the human location and safety zone, including a step of determining with the safety control module a blind capture set corresponding to the largest area the human or associated work object could move as a function of time. Claim 11. The method of Claim 10, wherein the robot assembly discontinues movement once the blind capture set overlaps an area in which the robot assembly is in operation. Claim 12. The method of Claim 5, including simultaneously developing a plurality of capture set areas for a plurality of human locations and developing an override path that avoids each capture set area. Claim 13. A robot assembly comprising: a C.P.U. including one or more computer-readable storage media storing computer- executable instructions being executed by one or more processors; a sensor for detecting a human location and human movement; a safety control module including a human location data and a safety zone data transmitted from the sensor; the human location data being associated with the human location; the safety zone data providing a boundary of a safety zone area that is associated with the human in a task oriented state that includes a largest possible area in which the human or an associated work object can extend when the human is standing in one location and performing the work task; and wherein the processor uses the safety zone data to avoid moving the robot assembly into the safety zone area. Claim 14. The robot assembly of Claim 13, including a moveable base to move the robot assembly between locations and wherein the safety zone data and the human location data are used to develop a capture set data including at least one predicted future 

 safety zone area location based on changes to the safety zone area as a function of time, and wherein the executable instructions include developing a travel path data for the robot assembly that avoids the capture set area. Claim 15. The robot assembly of Claim 14, wherein the travel path data includes instructions for a route of travel and the speed of the robot assembly to travel along the route.
